rulings.Case Title: JAMES TRUITT JR VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Compel Deft Nacco Materials Handling Group, Inc'S Person Most Knowledgeable And Custodian Of Records And Request For Production Of Documents (Inadvertently Taken Off Calendar)
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Tuesday, March 2, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:00 a.m., Line 3. Plaintiffs¿ motion to compel the deposition of defendant NACCO¿s person most knowledgeable and custodian of records and to compel the production of documents was continued from February 18, 2010 on the court¿s own motion and from February 23, 2010 at the parties¿ request. If the parties have not fully resolved all issues raised by the motion, they must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4pm today stating, without further argument, what issues remain for resolution. In the event that the parties have not informally resolved all issues raised by the motion, the parties must appear at the hearing on March 2. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JOANNE SETTERBERG et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Swagelock Companys Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Discovery Responses And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Tuesday, March 2, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:00 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Swagelok Company¿s motion to compel further discovery responses and request for sanctions is granted in part and denied in part. Plaintiff is required to respond to the discovery at issue based on all information available to her including Mr. Setterberg¿s deposition and VA records. In keeping with this admonition, plaintiff¿s responses to RFAs 1-8 and 12-13 are sufficient since plaintiff states that she has no basis to know the authenticity of the applications, but she is required to provide further responses to RFAs 9-11 which refer to VA actions and do not depend on the authenticity of the applications. Sanctions are denied. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: IN RE: PETITION OF GERALDINE HILT
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing Re: Amended Petition Of Geraldine Hilt To Perpetuate Testiony Of Robert Hilt By Videotaped Deposition
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Tuesday, March 2, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:00 a.m., Line 4. Petitioner¿s motion to take the deposition of Robert Hilt was continued from February 25, 2010 to provide counsel for petitioner and counsel for Dana Companies and Foster-Wheeler an opportunity to meet and confer regarding the motion in light of the fact that Mr. Hilt was deposed by his counsel in the personal injury action. If the parties have not fully resolved all issues raised by the motion, they must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:00 p.m. today stating, without further argument, what issues remain for resolution. In the event that the parties have not informally resolved all issues raised by the motion, the parties must appear at the hearing on March 2. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Calendar Matter: For Hearing On Order To Show Cause Re: Contempt As To Defendant Advocate Mines, Limited'S Compliance With Asbestos General Order No. 129
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Tuesday, March 2, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:00 a.m., Line 2. For Hearing On Order To Show Cause Re: Contempt As To Defendant Advocate Mines, Limited's Compliance With Asbestos General Order No. 129 is continued to March 11, 2010 at 9:00 a.m. in Department 220 per ex parte order filed on February 23. = (220/HEK)
